subject: grammar
title: Linking Words: Best Endings
instruction: Choose the best endings for these sentences based on the linking words used.

questions.text: I hurt my knee quite badly so...

options.A: I had to go to hospital.
options.B: I didn't have the right running shoes.

questions.text: One way to improve your health is to eat more fruit and vegetables. Alternatively,

options.A: you could increase your risk of heart disease.

questions.text: Humans like to form social groups. Likewise,

options.A: many other mammals live independently of each other.
options.B: many other mammals live together in small communities.

questions.text: I didn't work hard at school. Consequently,

options.B: I failed most of my exams.

questions.text: I believe travelling can help us to understand other cultures. Furthermore,

options.A: it can help you to become more independent.
options.B: it can be a lonely experience at times.

questions.text: In spite of the fact that learning to speak a foreign language requires a lot of time and effort,

options.A: many people give up after a short time.

questions.text: In my experience, vegetarians tend to be quite healthy. In contrast,

options.A: many meat-eaters do not have a healthy diet.
options.B: they look after their bodies well.

questions.text: Fifty per cent of all newspaper readers in this country only read the sports section of the paper. In other words,

options.A: many people are not interested in news.
options.B: sports are reported very well in the newspaper.

questions.text: Sydney is the biggest city in Australia, yet
You wouldn't be allowed to drive in my country because

options.A: there is no point in getting a car.

questions.text: Studies suggest that although sales of cookery books have increased significantly in the past five years,

options.A: we spend less time than ever in the kitchen.

questions.text: There are delays on all trains due to the fact that

options.B: there is a shortage of drivers.
